name: Maili Point
tagline: A low-key Waianae reef that delivers when you want to avoid the tourist trail entirely.
parking: Street parking along Farrington Hwy near Maili Beach Park
bestTime: Summer mornings; only snorkel when the ocean is calm — exposed reef with no protection
talkStory: Maili takes its name from the maile vine sacred in Hawaiian lei-making, and this Waianae coastline was once prime fishing grounds for local ahupuaa families. The reef harbors big schools of manini (convict tang) and kole (goldring surgeonfish) because fishing pressure is lower than the tourist corridor. On calm summer days the outer edge drops to 25 feet where ulua (giant trevally) cruise past. Respect the community — this is not a tourist beach.
